verdicts.rationale: raw/docs/01-what-is-abapgit.md:12-22 states abapGit is a git client for importing/exporting ABAP code between systems with plain-text repositories reviewable before import; research purpose file lines 26-31 conclude it serves the development/change-management process, not an end-user business process. Evidence states the whole sentence; consistent with L1 'What it does (business view)'.

Owner expert-answer line 17 (g002) states verbatim that in this installation it is not a productive client but the input of a public demo/benchmark, downloaded from docs.abapgit.org on 2026-07-02, with ZABAPGIT package and single-row TADIR as synthetic fixtures from demo/model-comparison/prepare.py. Consistent with the L1 frontmatter and the fixture-inventory research.

raw/docs/02-install-standalone.md:20-25 states the standalone is executed via SE38 and the developer version via transaction ZABAPGIT; research trigger file lines 30-35 conclude interactive SE38 launch, no own transaction code, no delivered scheduled trigger. Not contradicted by L1: the sy-batch background mode is an optional mode, not a delivered scheduled job.
Owner expert-answer line 21 (g004) states the program is never executed or scheduled in this installation: no SM37 jobs, no background processing. Directly proves the sentence.

raw/docs/01-what-is-abapgit.md:19-20 (developers with system access) plus research purpose file lines 33-34 which state explicitly 'there is no business-department end user'. Fully supports the sentence.

wiki-functional-semantics research lines 16-21/32-34 give the TYPE/VALUE/DATA_STR semantics and the seven c_type_* categories verbatim; matches the L1 'Output mapping' rows exactly (no contradiction).

wiki-functional-semantics research lines 36-40 (g7) state verbatim that 'DBT'='ZABAPGIT' is the documented emergency/recovery switch bypassing the HTML GUI to open the persistence DB utility for repair; consistent with L1 'Modes' (documented recovery mode, CL-004) and use case 5 (emergency repair of persistence).

wiki-lock-update-task-rule research lines 30-33 (g8) state the rule 'hold the ZABAPGIT persistence lock exactly until the caller's next commit' and that the FM names carry no business meaning (BUG-002 SMELL). Consistent with L1 Error handling and Bug candidates BUG-002.

research purpose file lines 36-40 (g9) plus raw/docs/01:19-22 state abapGit complements CTS as a review-friendly plain-text channel and that inside transportable packages changes still register with CTS via RS_CORR_INSERT. Consistent with L1 'External dependencies' (RS_CORR_INSERT = transport/correction registration).

research usage file lines 37-41 (g15) conclude two repository modes (online git over HTTP(S) requiring connectivity+SSL, and offline ZIP-based exchange for restricted/air-gapped networks); raw/docs/04-offline-projects.md:14-23 supports the offline part. Consistent with L1 use cases 1-4.

raw/docs/02-install-standalone.md:14-16 states SSL for online features, best with SAP GUI for Windows, BASIS 702+; research trigger file lines 37-39 (g11) restate with the STRUST gloss. Fully supports the sentence.

workspace-fixture-inventory research lines 28-32 (g12) with the inventory at lines 13-26 show none of the four user-exit includes exists as a real object (only dependency-derived _TMP_ stubs); because they use INCLUDE ... IF FOUND (L1 Extension points, CL-015) their absence is legal. No contradiction with L1.

Claim status is 'inferred'. workspace-fixture-inventory lines 33-35 mark it [INFERRED] per upstream abapGit design: with the authorization exit absent zcl_abapgit_auth=>is_allowed grants startup to anyone who can run the report. Reasonable per rule 6 but not provable from the snapshot code (the is_allowed default is not a code-verified fact in the L1 page); capped at partially_supported. Not contradicted by L1.

wiki-functional-semantics research lines 42-47 (g10) state the table is created and populated exclusively by abapGit (auto-created by first-run migration, only reader/writer via dynamic SQL on c_tabname), content = repo metadata/settings/checksums/user preferences, no external feeder or retention beyond its DB utility. Matches L1 Executive summary and External dependencies.

workspace-fixture-inventory research lines 37-41 (g14) state table ZABAPGIT was neither transported nor runtime-created in this snapshot (no TABL row, no DDIC source) and would be auto-created only at first run; owner expert-answer line 21 confirms the report never ran. Consistent with L1 (CL-018 auto-create on first run).

Owner expert-answer line 25 (g013) states no scheduled upgrade policy, manual refresh on upstream change, and the 2026-07-02 snapshot authoritative for published results; version-identity research lines 24-28 confirm abapGit 1.133.0 merged upstream 2026-07-01. Consistent with L1 Executive summary (CL-001).
